## Summary

<!--
One to three bullets. Lead with the "why", not the "what" — the diff
shows what changed; the PR body explains the motivation a reviewer
cannot reconstruct from `git diff`. Examples of good "why" lines:
"prevents drift between the README pin and package.json", "closes a
stale promise in dependencies.md", "fixes a future-tense statement
that survived after the decision was made".
-->

-

## Test plan

<!--
Checklist of concrete steps a reviewer can run to verify the change.
Each item should be a command, a file to read, or a behavior to
exercise — never a generic "tests pass" or "CI green" (those are
table stakes, not verification). Use checkboxes so the author can
self-check before requesting review.
-->

- [ ]
